conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me start by asking you some questions to assess your eligibility.

First, do you have any children you're asking about for Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child - my son Alex. He's 17 years old and lives with me at home. I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for him?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand a bit more about his current situation.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training? For example, is he studying at school, college, or in an approved training program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is! He's still in education - he's doing his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. A few more questions to make sure I have a complete picture of your situation.

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is there anyone else who might also consider themselves responsible for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me - he lives with me full-time and I'm responsible for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. One final question to complete my assessment:

Has Alex started any paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or is he claiming any benefits in his own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't. He doesn't work 24 hours a week or anything like that, and he's not claiming any benefits himself. He's just focused on his studies at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 17 years old and still in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college)
- You can continue to receive Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 as long as they remain in approved education or training
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for him
- He's not working 24+ hours per week or claiming benefits in his own right
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

All the eligibility criteria are met, so you should be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply online or by post. The benefit will continue as long as Alex stays in approved education or training, up until he turns 20.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: 1) He is 17 years old, which is over 16 but under 20; 2) He is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college); 3) He lives with you in the UK (Manchester); 4) You are responsible for him; 5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him; 6) He has not started paid work for 24 hours or more per week; 7) He is not claiming benefits in his own right. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, but you'll also still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. All conditions are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. Alex meets all the criteria: he is under 20, in approved education (A-levels), lives with the claimant in the UK, is not working 24+ hours per week, is not claiming benefits in his own right, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant is responsible for Alex and lives in the UK.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: 1) He is 17 years old, which is over 16 but under 20; 2) He is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college); 3) He lives with you in the UK (Manchester); 4) You are responsible for him; 5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him; 6) He has not started paid work for 24 hours or more per week; 7) He is not claiming benefits in his own right. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, but you'll also still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. All conditions are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. Alex meets all the criteria: he is under 20, in approved education (A-levels), lives with the claimant in the UK, is not working 24+ hours per week, is not claiming benefits in his own right, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant is responsible for Alex and lives in the UK.
